chapter_title.en: The Obligation of the jumu’ah Prayer, Its Virtue, Who is Exempt From it, and the Prayer and Sermon Therein

text: : «إِنَّ اَلْعَبْدَ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ لَيَسْأَلُ اَللَّهَ جَلَّ جَلاَلُهُ اَلْحَاجَةَ فَيُؤَخِّرُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَضَاءَ حَاجَتِهِ اَلَّتِي سَأَلَ إِلَى يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ لِيَخُصَّهُ بِفَضْلِ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1243 - Abu Basir narrated from one of the Imams (as) who said:
"Indeed, when a believing servant asks Allah (swt), Glorious and Majestic, for something, Allah (swt) may delay the fulfilment of his request until Jumu’ah in order to grant it to him with the special merit of the day of Jumu’ah."
